name: Strategic Bus Solutions NJ
districts_or_customers: Possibly NJ districts in Essex County area; not listed in NJDOE 2020-21 contractor codes located
recent_news: No M&A or news found; company not visible in NJ School Bus Contractors Association listings or NJ DOE public contractor codes - may operate under different DBA or be a small/recent entrant
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-site NJ operator at $929K revenue / 18 employees; low online footprint raises verification risk. Only attractive if confirmed as licensed NJ Type I/II contractor with real district routes - otherwise pass.

name: Johnsons School Bus
districts_or_customers: Unknown; Lubbock ISD is served by Durham School Services (Summit/National Express platform), and Lubbock-Cooper ISD runs its own transportation - leaving limited contracted district demand in the immediate Lubbock area
recent_news: No news located. Listed website 'Woodhousespas.Com' is clearly a data error (Woodhouse Spas is an unrelated retail spa chain). Cannot confirm this company as an active school-bus operating contractor
website: unverified (record lists Woodhousespas.Com which is incorrect)
fit_notes: Cannot validate as a genuine independent school-bus contractor. Wisconsin's Johnson School Bus Service is a different company. Lubbock ISD already contracted to Durham (Summit's platform), so even if real, addressable revenue is limited. Recommend deprioritize pending direct confirmation.

name: Holston Bus Co Inc
principals: Kenneth A. (Ken/Kenny) Morrell (Owner); Kenny Morrell (Office Manager, likely next-gen)
districts_or_customers: Bristol Tennessee City Schools (retained); previously served Sullivan County Schools (Morrell publicly ended that contract years ago)
recent_news: Owner Ken Morrell publicly explained ending Sullivan County service (Bristol Herald-Courier article, ~2014). Active and hiring per website. Founder has 57+ years of bus operating experience - clear succession/exit candidate.
fit_notes: Classic Summit tuck-in profile: 49-year-old family-owned single-site TN operator with anchor Bristol City Schools contract, founder of advanced age, perfect safety record per founder. Small ($800K-$900K revenue) but in-state for Summit's TN footprint. High succession signal makes outreach worthwhile.

name: Harlows School Bus Svc & Sales
owner_or_parent: Harlow's School Bus Service, Inc. of Montana (multi-state Harlow's organization, HQ Bismarck, ND)
principals: Harlow family (multi-generational); Jack Hellbusch is McCall site Manager/Route Manager (not owner)
fleet_size_est: ~400+ buses across the multi-state Harlow's network (McCall site itself is small, ~20 employees)
districts_or_customers: Historically McCall-Donnelly Joint School District (lost July 2025 - district took service in-house after contract dispute / separation agreement). McCall site also services river rafting outfitters, weddings, and tours.
recent_news: July 2025: McCall-Donnelly School District separated from Harlow's and took bus operations in-house for the first time in decades after a contract dispute. Materially weakens McCall site's contracted school revenue.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target - this McCall record is a single site of a large multi-state Harlow's organization (operations across ID, MT, ND, SD, WA, NE; ~30 locations; 400+ buses; school bus dealership business). Parent Harlow's is itself the kind of regional platform Summit might pursue at platform-level, but the McCall row alone is not actionable. Compounding negative: McCall lost its anchor district contract July 2025.

name: Checker Bus Of Marquette
owner_or_parent: independent (Checker Bus LLC / Checker Transport, Marquette MI)
principals: Emily St. Onge (General Manager per BUSRide profile); owner not publicly disclosed
fleet_size_est: 30+ vehicles total (sedans, vans, mini-coaches, limo buses, trolleys, motorcoaches, plus some school buses)
employees_est: ~25 drivers + 4 maintenance techs (~20 in source record)
districts_or_customers: Primarily charter/wedding/tour/limo/taxi work across Upper Peninsula Michigan and upper Midwest; school bus presence is a small portion of fleet, no confirmed district contracts located
recent_news: Profiled by BUSRide for adopting busHive operations software; UMA member; positions itself as upper-Midwest charter/motorcoach provider with diversified luxury fleet
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit. This is fundamentally a charter/motorcoach/limo/taxi operator with only incidental school-bus capacity, not a contracted yellow-bus operator. Scale (~30 mixed vehicles) and revenue mix do not match Summit's school-bus tuck-in thesis. Deprioritize.
